skill.name: on-call-handoff-patterns
skill.description: Create reliable on-call handoffs with structured context transfer, escalation reminders, incident status, and shift documentation templates.
skill.summary: Create clear on-call shift handoffs that preserve incident context and next actions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline backticks, ordinary words, and example checklists. The only retained risk factor is network because the skill includes hardcoded documentation, dashboard, and vendor links; no evidence of malicious execution, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Reference Links
low_findings.description: The skill contains example links to dashboards, internal documentation placeholders, Slack, PagerDuty, and public on-call resources. These are user-facing references in Markdown content, not automatic network requests or exfiltration paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible in Markdown link text and resource sections. They do not execute automatically, but they are real external or placeholder network destinations that users may open.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks and Code Fences
low_findings.description: Static analysis classified Markdown backticks and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The content is instructional Markdown and template text, with no runnable script file or command substitution logic in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown inline code or fenced documentation examples. No evidence found of Ruby code, backtick command substitution, or automatic execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at lines that contain ordinary prose about mastering handoffs and Kubernetes cluster changes. No cryptographic operation, hash function, encryption choice, or security-sensitive algorithm appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line content is unrelated to cryptography. The scanner likely matched substrings inside normal English words.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Scheduling and Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged an at-command scheduling pattern and network reconnaissance patterns in checklist or status text. The lines describe a status page update, database access verification, and routine documentation work, not scheduled command execution or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens occur in operational checklist prose. No evidence found of at jobs, port scanning, host discovery, or reconnaissance commands.

content.value_statement: On-call transitions often lose incident context, owners, and next actions. This skill gives teams structured handoff templates and escalation patterns for safer shift changes.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured on-call handoff documents for active incidents and investigations.
Provides quick async handoff and mid-incident handoff templates.
Defines pre-shift, daily, and post-shift on-call checklists.
Organizes recent changes, known issues, upcoming events, and escalation reminders.
Suggests a 15-minute handoff sync agenda for outgoing and incoming responders.
limitations: Does not connect to PagerDuty, Slack, Grafana, or ticketing systems automatically.
Does not verify live incident status or service health without user-provided context.
Example links and team names must be replaced with organization-specific resources.
Does not replace incident command, legal review, or security escalation policies.

use_cases.title: Prepare Weekly Rotation Handoffs
use_cases.description: Create a complete shift summary with active issues, recent changes, risks, and upcoming maintenance.

use_cases.title: Transfer Incidents Mid-Response
use_cases.description: Summarize current state, actions taken, remaining work, and escalation contacts during an active incident.

use_cases.title: Standardize On-Call Onboarding
use_cases.description: Give new responders repeatable checklists for access, alerting, daily routines, and escalation decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Shift Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner handoff for a normal rotation change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft an on-call handoff. Include active incidents, ongoing investigations, recent changes, known issues, upcoming events, and escalation contacts.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize an Active Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate handoff when an issue is unresolved.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn these investigation notes into a handoff. Highlight impact, evidence, current theory, next checks, owner, and when to escalate.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Mid-Incident Transfer
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff during an ongoing incident.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a mid-incident handoff. Include timeline, current mitigation, remaining risk, communications status, key people, and incoming responder confirmations.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Team Handoff Process
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced process design for a team rotation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit our current on-call handoff process. Identify missing context, weak escalation paths, checklist gaps, and a practical improved workflow.

output_examples.input: A platform team needs to hand off a quiet week with one API latency investigation and a release tomorrow.
output_examples.output: A concise handoff with no active incidents, one watch item, recent deployments, release monitoring notes, dashboard references, and incoming engineer checklist items.

output_examples.input: An incident commander must leave during a payment service degradation.
output_examples.output: A mid-incident transfer summary covering severity, current mitigation, known root cause signals, customer communications, owners, and clear next decisions.

output_examples.input: A manager wants a repeatable checklist for new on-call engineers.
output_examples.output: A pre-shift and daily routine checklist covering access, alert delivery, dashboards, runbooks, escalation contacts, and end-of-shift documentation.

best_practices: Separate facts, theories, actions taken, and next steps so incoming responders can trust the handoff quickly.
Use a short synchronous overlap for active incidents or high-risk investigations.
Replace example links, teams, and thresholds with current internal systems before operational use.
anti_patterns: Do not hand off only in chat when an issue needs durable context and ownership.
Do not omit recent deployments, configuration changes, or maintenance windows from a shift summary.
Do not treat the template as live status without verifying current alerts and incident records.

faq.question: Can this skill create a full on-call handoff document?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides sections for incidents, investigations, changes, known issues, upcoming events, and escalation reminders.

faq.question: Does it integrate with monitoring or paging tools?
faq.answer: No. It provides templates and process guidance, but users must supply data from their tools.

faq.question: Is it useful during an active incident?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a mid-incident handoff pattern for transferring state, owners, decisions, and remaining work.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should teams customize before using it?
faq.answer: Teams should update service names, escalation paths, dashboards, runbooks, severity rules, and response targets.

faq.question: Does it make operational decisions automatically?
faq.answer: No. It structures human decision making and documentation. Responders remain responsible for verification and escalation.
